title: Transcribe a Voice Memo to Text Using On-Device Recognition
creator: [You]

userStory.persona: a user who recorded a voice memo
userStory.want: my memo to be accurately transcribed into readable text entirely on my Mac
userStory.soThat: the content of what I said is captured in written form without relying on any cloud service

context: Transcription uses macOS 26 SFSpeechRecognizer with on-device recognition. The spike confirmed that .m4a files are supported and established the expected performance profile. The transcription engine is stateless — it receives a file path and returns a result struct containing the text, confidence, duration, and any error.

acceptanceCriteria.then: it returns the transcribed text, a confidence score between 0 and 1, and the audio duration in seconds

acceptanceCriteria.given: a 1-minute voice memo recorded in a quiet environment with clear English speech
acceptanceCriteria.then: the transcribed text is at least 95% accurate when compared word-by-word to the spoken content
transcription completes within 2 minutes (adjust threshold based on spike findings)

acceptanceCriteria.given: a voice memo that contains extended silence or only background noise
acceptanceCriteria.then: it returns empty or near-empty text with a low confidence score and does not fail or hang

acceptanceCriteria.given: an audio file that exceeds the maximum duration supported by the API (if a limit was identified in the spike)
acceptanceCriteria.when: the transcription engine attempts to process it
acceptanceCriteria.then: it returns a clear error indicating the duration limit was exceeded, including the file's duration and the maximum allowed

acceptanceCriteria.given: a corrupted .m4a file that cannot be decoded
acceptanceCriteria.when: the transcription engine attempts to process it
acceptanceCriteria.then: it returns a clear error indicating the file could not be read, and does not crash or hang

acceptanceCriteria.given: a file path that no longer exists (the file was deleted after detection but before transcription)
acceptanceCriteria.when: the transcription engine attempts to process it
acceptanceCriteria.then: it returns a file-not-found error with the missing path included in the error message
all system resources (file handles, audio sessions, recognition tasks) are released and not leaked

outOfScope: Deciding what to do with the transcribed text, updating state, retry logic. This story is a pure function: file path in, result out. Integration with the pipeline is Story 5.
